description: GraphRAG frozen benchmark with 60 questions: easy, medium, hard, architecture, debugging/governance.

questions.query: What does RAGPipeline.ask do?
Where is query intent detected?
Which file defines vector store factory selection?
How is the graph loaded on startup?
Which endpoint answers AI mentor questions?
Where are embeddings generated for nodes?
What is the default embedding model?
Where are prompt templates for governance questions?
What function builds condensed graph hierarchy?
Where is blast radius explanation generated?
Which file calculates architectural drift metrics?
Where is graph summary context built for AI?
How does the backend keep AI optional when dependencies fail?
Explain how vector index refresh works after repository upload.
How are graph entities extracted from retrieval metadata?
How does context aggregation vary by query intent?
Where is code complexity surfaced to AI responses?
How does graph blast radius calculate upstream and downstream impact?
How is CORS configured from environment variables?
How does Smart Blame bus factor endpoint determine risk areas?
Where is repository path inferred from parsed entities?
How are class inheritance relationships represented in the graph?
How does Chroma recovery handle corrupted persistence state?
How does governance drift compare current metrics to baseline?
Trace the full path from /ai/ask request to final LLM response assembly.
Explain how graph context, feature data, and code context are combined under latency constraints.
How does fallback behavior differ between AI initialization failure and retrieval miss?
Describe all risk factors used in blast radius scoring and where each signal comes from.
How does call resolution disambiguate self, super, imports, and local file calls?
Explain the data lifecycle from parser output to graph store and then vector index.
Where are graph edges filtered for condensed hierarchy and why?
How does architecture validation expose violations and warnings through API contracts?
Compare retrieval semantics between Chroma and OpenSearch adapters.
What error containment exists for pyo3 panics and storage corruption?
How does repository upload trigger refresh of AI and git analyzers safely?
Describe how Smart Blame combines weighted factors into expertise score.
Explain the responsibilities of backend/ai, backend/graph, backend/governance, and backend/git.
What are the layering boundaries enforced by governance rules?
How does the API maintain a single in-memory source of truth for graph data?
How are pluggable backends handled for graph and vector stores?
Which parts are synchronous vs asynchronous in the AI request path?
How does the system support both frontend web and terminal UI clients?
Where can architecture drift baseline be persisted and reloaded?
How is dependency graph metadata merged with raw entity data for API responses?
Explain how governance endpoints map to validator and drift detector internals.
How does upload status tracking coordinate parsing and graph rebuilding phases?
What are the key observability metrics currently available by design docs?
How does API info endpoint expose Smart Blame factor weights?
Why might /ai/ask return no relevant context even when code exists?
What causes graph hierarchy tests to fail when repository path changes?
How can stale vector index happen and how is it detected?
What are likely failure points in governance drift detection endpoint?
How does AI module disablement affect endpoint behavior?
Why would Smart Blame tests fail with missing blame module imports?
Where to inspect if OpenSearch vector search returns empty results?
How to diagnose CORS issues from frontend to backend?
What fallback happens when multi-source RAG context building throws exceptions?
How does parser skip directories and why could entities be missing?
What governance signal indicates architecture drift got worse?
How can corrupted Chroma persistence be recovered safely?
